T0338.title: Card-and-PIN Redemption
T0338.definition: In money laundering operations, card-and-PIN write-off refers to the act of binding illegally obtained electronic card numbers and PINs to accounts or consuming them to realize virtual assets.
T0338.description: After purchasing electronic cards with fraudulent funds, downstream card-and-PIN write-off agents bind the card credentials to designated apps or platform accounts, converting them into usable balances or services. This process often involves e-commerce cards, gaming top-up cards, prepaid cards, and other virtual vouchers. Tasks are distributed through instant messaging groups or darknet postings seeking card-and-PIN recyclers. The practice transforms illicit proceeds into virtual assets that are difficult to trace, representing a common cleansing stage in money-muling and money-laundering schemes.
T0338.usageExample: Anyone who can buy shopping cards, come through. Shopping card-and-PIN write-off: you get 650 per recycled card.
